title: Add immutable field blocklist to update_fields() repository methods
description:
SEVERITY: Medium (security hardening)
REVIEW ID: MED-07, SEC-1
FILES: src/core/database/repositories/media_buy.py:380-395, src/core/database/repositories/product.py:138-153

update_fields() uses setattr(model, key, value) without checking if the field is immutable. A caller could pass tenant_id='other_tenant' and move a record to a different tenant.

No current exploit path (all callers use hard-coded field names), but the interface doesn't prevent it.

FIX: Add blocklist:
```python
_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS = frozenset({'tenant_id', 'media_buy_id', 'created_at'})

for key, value in kwargs.items():
    if key in _IMMUTABLE_FIELDS:
        raise ValueError(f'Cannot update immutable field {key!r}')
```

Also apply to update_package_fields() at line 448.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] tenant_id, primary keys, and created_at blocked from update
- [ ] ValueError raised with clear message
- [ ] Existing callers unaffected
- [ ] make quality green
notes:
## Prep
- Files to modify: src/core/database/repositories/media_buy.py, src/core/database/repositories/product.py
- Three update methods need blocklist guards:
  1. MediaBuyRepository.update_fields() (line 380) — immutable: tenant_id, media_buy_id, created_at
  2. MediaBuyRepository.update_package_fields() (line 448) — immutable: media_buy_id, package_id
  3. ProductRepository.update_fields() (line 138) — immutable: tenant_id, product_id
- Approach: Add _IMMUTABLE_FIELDS frozenset as class constant on each repo. Check kwargs against it before setattr loop. Raise ValueError with clear message.
## Execution
- Changes made:
  - MediaBuyRepository: Added _MEDIA_BUY_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS (tenant_id, media_buy_id, created_at) and _PACKAGE_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS (media_buy_id, package_id) frozensets. Added blocklist check to update_fields() and update_package_fields().
  - ProductRepository: Added _IMMUTABLE_FIELDS (tenant_id, product_id) frozenset. Added blocklist check to update_fields().
- All three methods raise ValueError before any DB lookup if caller passes immutable fields.
- make quality: PASS (3818 passed, formatting/lint/mypy clean on modified files)

title: Integration-1: Fix teardown + setup constraint violations exposed by Alembic templates (50+ tests)
description:
Switching integration tests to Alembic template databases exposed 50+ tests with invalid data and broken teardown. Previously invisible because Base.metadata.create_all() didn't install triggers/constraints.

## 6 Root Causes

### RC-1: prevent_empty_pricing_options trigger at TEARDOWN (~27 tests)
Teardown fixtures do 'DELETE FROM pricing_options WHERE tenant_id = X' — bulk delete hits the trigger when deleting the last pricing option for a product.
Fix: Don't delete pricing options individually. Delete the PRODUCT (which has passive_deletes=True → CASCADE at DB level → trigger sees product gone → allows delete). Or better: don't delete anything — the per-test cloned DB is dropped after the test.
Files: Various integration test conftest.py teardown fixtures, tests/integration/conftest.py

### RC-2: FK principals_tenant_id_fkey at TEARDOWN (3 tests)  
Teardown tries DELETE FROM tenants but principals still reference the tenant.
Fix: Delete in correct FK order (principals before tenants), or use CASCADE, or don't delete (DB is dropped).
Files: tests/admin/test_product_creation_integration.py teardown

### RC-3: NOT NULL tenant_id at TEARDOWN (5 tests)
Teardown does UPDATE media_buys SET tenant_id=None — NOT NULL constraint blocks.
Fix: Don't null out FK columns before delete. Delete the media_buy directly, or don't delete (DB is dropped).
Files: tests/integration/test_format_conversion_approval.py teardown

### RC-4: check_auction_has_price_guidance at SETUP (11 tests)
Fixture creates PricingOption(is_fixed=False, rate=10.00, price_guidance=None). DB requires auction options to have price_guidance.
Fix: Add price_guidance to the fixture: price_guidance={'floor': 1.0, 'p25': 2.0, 'p50': 3.0, 'p75': 4.0, 'p90': 5.0}
Files: tests/integration/test_product_deletion.py fixture

### RC-5: validate_format_ids trigger at SETUP (1 test)
Product created with format_ids=[{'format_id':'display_300x250','name':'Display 300x250','type':'display'}]. DB trigger requires {'agent_url': ..., 'id': ...} shape.
Fix: Use correct format_ids shape: [{'agent_url': 'https://creative.adcontextprotocol.org', 'id': 'display_300x250'}]
Files: tests/integration/test_gam_pricing_models_integration.py fixture (or wherever TestGAMProductConfiguration sets up product data)

### RC-6: FK audit_logs_tenant_id_fkey at TEARDOWN (6 tests)
Production code creates audit_log entries. Teardown can't delete tenant because audit_logs reference it.
Fix: Same as RC-2 — delete in correct FK order or don't delete (DB is dropped).
Files: tests/integration/test_product_format_validation.py, tests/integration/test_update_media_buy_persistence.py teardowns

## Meta-Fix: Stop deleting in teardown
ALL of RC-1, RC-2, RC-3, RC-6 are teardown problems. The per-test database is CLONED from an Alembic template and DROPPED after the test. Manual teardown (DELETE statements) is unnecessary and actively harmful — it hits triggers and FK constraints.

The correct pattern: integration_db fixture handles DB lifecycle. Tests create data via factories. No manual cleanup in teardown. The DB is dropped automatically.

For any fixture that currently has teardown code (yield → cleanup), remove the cleanup section. The integration_db fixture's finalizer handles it.

## HANG issue: External HTTP in integration tests
Removing the ADCP_TESTING bypass means _list_creative_formats_impl now calls the real creative agent at DEFAULT_AGENT.agent_url. Default is https://creative.adcontextprotocol.org. Integration tests hang waiting for this external HTTP call.

Fix: CREATIVE_AGENT_URL env var must be set for ALL test suites pointing at the Docker creative agent container. scripts/test-stack.sh already exports MOCK_CREATIVE_AGENT_URL but not CREATIVE_AGENT_URL.

Add to scripts/test-stack.sh:
    export CREATIVE_AGENT_URL="http://localhost:${MOCK_AGENT_PORT}"

And to tox.ini pass_env:
    CREATIVE_AGENT_URL

This way the DEFAULT_AGENT in creative_agent_registry.py reads the env var and points at the Docker container instead of the real internet URL.

## Reproduction
./run_all_tests.sh  (will show errors + hang)

## Files to Modify
- scripts/test-stack.sh (add CREATIVE_AGENT_URL export)
- tox.ini (add CREATIVE_AGENT_URL to pass_env)
- tests/integration/conftest.py (check/remove teardown cleanup)
- tests/integration/test_product_deletion.py (fix auction pricing fixture: add price_guidance)
- tests/integration/test_format_conversion_approval.py (remove teardown that nulls tenant_id)
- tests/integration/test_gam_pricing_models_integration.py (fix format_ids shape + teardown)
- tests/admin/test_product_creation_integration.py (remove teardown)
- tests/integration/test_product_format_validation.py (remove teardown)
- tests/integration/test_update_media_buy_persistence.py (remove teardown)

## Core Invariant
Integration tests run against a disposable per-test DB clone. No manual teardown. Factories create valid data respecting all DB constraints. The DB is dropped automatically — no DELETE statements needed.

title: Phase A: UC-026 harness env for keyword/targeting scenarios

title: E2E: UC-005 REST xfail XPASS(strict) — Docker server supports filters (8 tests)
description:
8 UC-005 e2e_rest tests fail as XPASS(strict). They're in _REST_XFAIL_TAGS with strict=True (reason: REST endpoint drops filter params). But on e2e_rest, the Docker server's REST endpoint actually implements the filters — so the tests PASS, violating the strict xfail.

## Failing tests (all e2e_rest, all XPASS strict)
- test_brrule031_inv1_violated (T-UC-005-inv-031-1-violated)
- test_brrule049_inv3_violated (T-UC-005-inv-049-3-violated)
- test_brrule049_inv4_violated (T-UC-005-inv-049-4-violated)
- test_brrule049_inv4_edge__nodim (T-UC-005-inv-049-4-nodim)
- test_brrule049_inv9_violated (T-UC-005-inv-049-9-violated)
- test_brrule049_inv9_edge__nofield (T-UC-005-inv-049-9-nofield)
- test_brrule049_inv10_violated (T-UC-005-inv-049-10-violated)
- test_brrule049_inv10_edge__nofield (T-UC-005-inv-049-10-nofield)

## Root cause
_REST_XFAIL_TAGS applies to both is_rest and is_e2e_rest with strict=True. The harness REST stub drops filter params (real gap). But e2e_rest goes through the Docker server which implements filters → tests pass → XPASS(strict) = failure.

## Fix
Split the xfail: keep strict=True for is_rest (harness stub), use strict=False for is_e2e_rest (Docker server may implement filters). Or remove e2e_rest from the guard entirely if the Docker server fully supports filters.

# Context Bundle: test_brrule031_inv1_violated__and_combination_excludes_partial_matches

**Tags**: `BR-RULE-031`, `T-UC-005-inv-031-1-violated`
**Transports failing**: a2a, e2e_rest, impl, mcp
**Step file**: `tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc005_creative_formats.py`
**Agent index**: `.agent-index/INDEX.md`

---

## Error

*(No error message captured — step body is likely a stub/no-op or missing assertion.)*

---

## Gherkin Scenario

*File: `tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-005-discover-creative-formats.feature`*

```gherkin
@T-UC-005-inv-031-1-violated @UC-005-MAIN-MCP-16 @invariant @BR-RULE-031
Scenario: BR-RULE-031 INV-1 violated - AND combination excludes partial matches
  Given the registry has format "pre-roll" of type "video" with asset type "video"
  When the Buyer Agent requests formats with type "display" and asset_types ["video"]
  Then no formats should be returned
  # BR-RULE-031 INV-1: type=display excludes video-type format despite matching asset_types

---

## adcp-req Scenario (upstream spec)

*Full feature file: `tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-005-discover-creative-formats.feature`*

```gherkin
# @contextgit id=T-UC-005-inv-031-1-violated type=test upstream=[BR-RULE-031]
@invariant @BR-RULE-031
Scenario: BR-RULE-031 INV-1 violated - AND combination excludes partial matches
  Given the registry has format "pre-roll" of type "video" with asset type "video"
  When the Buyer Agent requests formats with type "display" and asset_types ["video"]
  Then no formats should be returned
  # BR-RULE-031 INV-1: type=display excludes video-type format despite matching asset_types

---

## Business Rule: BR-RULE-031

*Full file: `docs/business-rules/BR-RULE-031.md`*

**Rule Statement**: All filter parameters in a format discovery request are optional and combine as AND — a format must satisfy every provided filter to be included (no filters returns the full catalog). Results are sorted by format type then name; individual filter semantics are defined in BR-RULE-049.

**Relevant Invariants** (verbatim):

| ID | Condition | Consequence |
|----|-----------|-------------|
| INV-1 | Multiple filters combined | All filters apply as AND — format must satisfy every provided filter |
| INV-2 | Any valid request | Results sorted by format type value then format name |

**What this scenario tests**: INV-1 — a format with `type="video"` must be excluded when `type="display"` is requested, even though it matches the `asset_types=["video"]` filter. Both conditions must be satisfied simultaneously.

---

## Orientation (MANDATORY — do this FIRST)

1. Read `.agent-index/INDEX.md` — this is the complete index of pre-computed type stubs. It tells you which file answers which question (harness envs, factories, schemas, errors).
2. Read the stub files listed in INDEX.md that are relevant to this UC.
3. Use `ast-grep --pattern '@then($_)' tests/bdd/steps/` to find existing step patterns.

DO NOT grep the codebase for harness methods. The .agent-index stubs are the complete API.
DO NOT invent mock hacks for methods not listed in the stubs.

---

## Conftest Harness Routing (which env class is used for this UC)

```python
uc == "UC-005":
        extra = _setup_db(request, ctx)
        from tests.harness.creative_formats import CreativeFormatsEnv

        with CreativeFormatsEnv(**extra) as env:
            ctx["env"] = env
            yield

The harness class for this scenario is **`CreativeFormatsEnv`** (from `tests/harness/creative_formats.py`). Read its stub in `.agent-index/` to discover available methods before writing the Then step.

---

## What the Then Step Must Assert

The step `Then no formats should be returned` must:
1. Retrieve the response/result from `ctx["env"]` (check the stub for the correct attribute/method)
2. Assert that the returned formats list is **empty** — length 0, not just falsy
3. Use a hard equality assertion (`assert len(...) == 0` or `assert result.formats == []`), not `assert not result` or `assert result is None`

Do **not** delegate to a no-op, do **not** use `pass`, do **not** check truthiness only.
## Findings
- tests/bdd/conftest.py:464 applies _REST_XFAIL_TAGS xfail(strict=True) to both is_rest and is_e2e_rest. Docker REST endpoint actually supports the filter params, so the 8 e2e_rest tests XPASS → strict xfail fails.
- nmg9 fix added separate e2e_rest xfail(strict=False) for overlapping T-UC-005 tags. On e2e_rest, both markers currently apply → strict=True wins and test fails.

## Core Invariant
strict=True requires guaranteed failure on that transport. e2e_rest doesn't share the REST-harness filter gap; only in-process REST does.

## Fix
Restrict _REST_XFAIL_TAGS application to is_rest only (drop is_e2e_rest). Leave the nmg9 e2e_rest xfail(strict=False) as the e2e_rest coverage (set_registry_formats sidecar gap).

title: E2E: UC-004 webhook/delivery assertions use mocks unavailable in e2e (7 scenarios)
description:
7 UC-004 e2e_rest scenarios fail because they assert on env.mock['post'] (webhook POST capture) or circuit breaker state, which don't exist in e2e mode — the real Docker server handles webhooks internally.

## Failing tests (all e2e_rest only)
- test_bearer_token_webhook_authentication
- test_hmacsha256_signed_webhook_payload
- test_webhook_notification_type__type
- test_webhook_payload_does_not_include_aggregated_totals
- test_persistent_webhook_failures_open_circuit_breaker
- test_circuit_breaker_closes_after_successful_recovery_probes
- test_successful_retry_records_delivery

## Root cause
Webhook Then steps read env.mock['post'].call_args for payload/header assertions. In e2e mode, the mock doesn't exist — the real server sends real HTTP POST to a real webhook URL. Circuit breaker state is internal to the server process.

## Fix options
A. xfail on e2e_rest with reason (webhook internals not observable via HTTP)
B. Set up a test webhook receiver that captures POST payloads for e2e verification

# Context Bundle: test_bearer_token_webhook_authentication

**Tags**: `@T-UC-004-webhook-bearer @alternative @webhook @invariant @BR-RULE-029`
**Step file**: `tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc004_delivery.py`
**Transports failing**: a2a, e2e_rest, impl, mcp, rest

---

## Error (VERBATIM)
AssertionError: No webhook POST call recorded
assert None is not None

---

## Gherkin Scenario (VERBATIM)
*File: `tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-004-deliver-media-buy-metrics.feature`*

```gherkin
@T-UC-004-webhook-bearer @alternative @webhook @invariant @BR-RULE-029
Scenario: Bearer token webhook authentication
  Given a media buy "mb-001" with webhook authentication scheme "Bearer"
  And the bearer token is a valid 32+ character string
  When the system delivers a webhook report for "mb-001"
  Then the request should include header "Authorization" with the bearer token
  # Webhook auth: traces to SR-NFR-005

---

## Business Rule: BR-RULE-029 (relevant invariants, VERBATIM)

> Authentication/signing of webhook payloads is a precondition handled by the general authentication infrastructure (see SR-NFR-005).

> **Note:** Webhook authentication/signing (HMAC-SHA256, Bearer) is a precondition for delivery, not a business rule of this domain. See SR-NFR-005 for auth requirements.

The Then step assertion must verify that the outgoing webhook HTTP request carries an `Authorization` header whose value is the bearer token.

Full business rule: `docs/` (search for `BR-RULE-029`)

---

## Harness: WebhookEnv

This scenario routes to `WebhookEnv` (the `"webhook"` branch in conftest). Note: no DB is set up — `WebhookEnv` is self-contained.

```python
elif harness_type == "webhook":
    from tests.harness.delivery_webhook import WebhookEnv

    with WebhookEnv() as env:
        ctx["env"] = env
        yield

`ctx["env"]` is a `WebhookEnv` instance. The Then step must interrogate this env to find the recorded outgoing webhook POST and assert on its headers.

Full harness: `tests/harness/delivery_webhook.py`

---

## Relevant Schema Fields

From `get-media-buy-delivery-response.json` — fields relevant to webhook delivery:
- `notification_type`: string
- `sequence_number`: integer

Authentication scheme used in Given step: `"Bearer"` (string literal — use exactly this value).

Full schemas:
- `get-media-buy-delivery-request.json`
- `get-media-buy-delivery-response.json`

---

## Orientation (VERBATIM — do this FIRST)

1. Read `.agent-index/INDEX.md` — this is the complete index of pre-computed type stubs.
   It tells you which file answers which question (harness envs, factories, schemas, errors).
2. Read the stub files listed in INDEX.md that are relevant to this UC.
3. Use `ast-grep --pattern '@then($_)' tests/bdd/steps/` to find existing step patterns.

DO NOT grep the codebase for harness methods. The .agent-index stubs are the complete API.
DO NOT invent mock hacks for methods not listed in the stubs.

---

## Conftest Harness Routing (VERBATIM)

```python
uc == "UC-004":
        harness_type = _detect_delivery_harness(request)

        if harness_type == "poll":
            extra = _setup_db(request, ctx)
            extra.setdefault("principal_id", "buyer-001")
            from tests.harness.delivery_poll import DeliveryPollEnv

            with DeliveryPollEnv(**extra) as env:
                tenant, principal = env.setup_default_data()
                ctx["env"] = env
                ctx["db_tenant"] = tenant
                ctx[f"db_principal_{env._principal_id}"] = principal
                yield
        elif harness_type == "webhook":
            from tests.harness.delivery_webhook import WebhookEnv

            with WebhookEnv() as env:
                ctx["env"] = env
                yield
        elif harness_type == "circuit-breaker":
            extra = _setup_db(request, ctx)
            from tests.harness.delivery_circuit_breaker import CircuitBreakerEnv

            with CircuitBreakerEnv(**extra) as env:
                tenant, principal = env.setup_default_data()
                ctx["env"] = env
                ctx["db_tenant"] = tenant
                ctx[f"db_principal_{env._principal_id}"] = principal
                yield

---

## What the Fix Likely Requires

The error `AssertionError: No webhook POST call recorded` points to the Then step (`the request should include header "Authorization" with the bearer token`) not finding any recorded outgoing request on the `WebhookEnv`.

Check in order:
1. **Does `WebhookEnv` expose a method to retrieve recorded calls?** Read `.agent-index/` stubs for `WebhookEnv` — find the exact method name and return type.
2. **Does the When step actually trigger a delivery?** If `WebhookEnv.deliver(...)` or equivalent is never called in the When step, the recorded calls list will be empty.
3. **Does the Then step retrieve the call correctly?** The assertion must be: `call.headers["Authorization"] == f"Bearer {token}"` (or whatever the env records).

Do NOT weaken the assertion to check for truthiness. The Gherkin says "with the bearer token" — the test must compare the actual header value against the token stored in `ctx`.
## Reproduction + Findings
- Local 4 transports: pass
- e2e_rest failures (per bug): webhook mock POST/circuit state not observable in Docker
- Tags: T-UC-004-webhook-bearer, webhook-hmac, webhook-notification-type, webhook-no-aggregated, webhook-circuit-open, webhook-circuit-recovery, webhook-retry-success
- Core Invariant: webhook internals (mock['post'], CircuitBreaker state) aren't observable through the real HTTP path — xfail e2e_rest with strict=False
close_reason: E2E_REST xfails added with strict=False; in-process coverage unchanged

title: Infra-4: UC-002/003 test-mode adapter — inject behavior config via DB (16 tests)
description:
16 E2E failures because Docker's mock adapter auto-approves everything and can't simulate failures. Tests need to control adapter behavior from the test process.

## Problem
UC-002 (12 E2E): adapter failure simulation, manual approval flow, workflow_step_id
UC-003 (4 E2E): manual approval — Expected 'submitted', got 'completed'

In-process tests configure env.mock['adapter'].manual_approval_required = True. In E2E, the mock is in the test process; Docker's adapter auto-approves.

## Architecture
The mock adapter in Docker already reads configuration from the database. Add a test_behavior mechanism:

### Option A: Use existing AdapterConfig table
AdapterConfig (src/core/database/models.py) has a JSON config column. Add a test_behavior key that the mock adapter checks:
    {
        'test_behavior': {
            'manual_approval_required': true,
            'fail_on_create': false,
            'error_message': null
        }
    }

Given steps write this via factory (through env._session → Docker DB). Docker's mock adapter reads adapter_config before each operation.

### Option B: New TestAdapterConfig table
Cleaner separation but more migration work. Probably overkill.

## Implementation Plan (Option A)

### 1. Mock adapter reads test_behavior
src/adapters/mock/adapter.py — in create_media_buy, update_media_buy:
    config = self._get_adapter_config()
    test_behavior = (config or {}).get('test_behavior', {})
    if test_behavior.get('manual_approval_required'):
        self.manual_approval_required = True
    if test_behavior.get('fail_on_create'):
        raise AdCPAdapterError(test_behavior.get('error_message', 'Test adapter failure'))

### 2. Factory/helper for test_behavior
tests/factories/core.py AdapterConfigFactory already exists. Add a helper:
    def set_adapter_test_behavior(env, **behavior):
        from tests.factories import AdapterConfigFactory
        AdapterConfigFactory(
            tenant=env._tenant,
            adapter_type='mock',
            config={'test_behavior': behavior}
        )
        env._commit_factory_data()

### 3. Given steps use the helper
tests/bdd/steps/generic/given_media_buy.py:
    @given('the ad server adapter requires manual approval')
    def given_manual_approval(ctx):
        set_adapter_test_behavior(ctx['env'], manual_approval_required=True)

This replaces: env.mock['adapter'].manual_approval_required = True (only works in-process)

## Failing Tests (16 E2E_REST)
UC-002: test_approval_workflow_*, test_persistence_timing_*, test_inv2_holds__adapter_failure_*, test_inv4_holds__transient_error, test_audit_logging, test_minimum_order_size, test_production_account_sandbox
UC-003: test_approval_workflow__adapter/tenant_requires_manual_review, test_adapter_atomicity__failure_rolls_back

## Reproduction
tox -e bdd -- tests/bdd/test_uc002_create_media_buy.py -k 'e2e_rest and (approval_workflow or persistence_timing or adapter_failure)' -x

## Files
- src/adapters/mock/adapter.py (read test_behavior from config)
- tests/factories/core.py AdapterConfigFactory (already exists — add helper)
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/given_media_buy.py (adapter config Given steps)
- tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc003_update_media_buy.py (same for update)

## Core Invariant
Test-controllable adapter behavior flows through the shared database, not cross-process mocking. Given steps write to DB → Docker adapter reads from DB. Same data path, same database.

title: Implement _list_accounts_impl + wrappers
description:
list_accounts tool per AdCP spec. Agent-scoped results, status filter, cursor pagination, anonymous = empty. MCP + A2A wrappers. GitHub #1011.

Business rules: BR-RULE-054, BR-RULE-055
Files: src/core/tools/accounts/_list.py, list_wrappers.py
design:
## Core Invariant
_list_accounts_impl is transport-agnostic: accepts ResolvedIdentity, uses AccountUoW for DB access, returns ListAccountsResponse — zero imports from fastmcp/a2a/starlette.

## Architecture Decisions
1. Single file src/core/tools/accounts.py (like properties.py — simple enough)
2. Unauthenticated → empty response (BR-RULE-055), not error
3. Authenticated → list_for_agent scoping (BR-RULE-054)
4. Status filter support via ListAccountsRequest.status
5. ORM→schema conversion inline (direct field mapping)

## Implementation Plan
1. Create src/core/tools/accounts.py:
   - _list_accounts_impl(req, identity) → ListAccountsResponse
   - list_accounts(ctx) → ToolResult (MCP wrapper)
   - list_accounts_raw(identity) → ListAccountsResponse (A2A wrapper)
2. Register in main.py (MCP tool)
3. Add to AUTH_OPTIONAL_TOOLS in mcp_auth_middleware.py
4. Add to tools/__init__.py re-exports
5. Wire A2A in adcp_a2a_server.py (handler + DISCOVERY_SKILLS + AgentSkill)
notes:
## Findings

### _impl + Wrapper Pattern (from properties.py)
- _impl: sync def, accepts ResolvedIdentity, uses UoW context manager, raises AdCPError, returns Pydantic model
- MCP wrapper: async def, gets identity from ctx.get_state('identity'), returns ToolResult
- A2A raw: sync def, accepts identity directly, calls _impl

### Registration Points
- MCP: src/core/main.py — import + mcp.tool()(with_error_logging(fn))
- MCP auth: src/core/mcp_auth_middleware.py — add to AUTH_OPTIONAL_TOOLS (BR-RULE-055)
- A2A: src/a2a_server/adcp_a2a_server.py — import raw, add skill handler + DISCOVERY_SKILLS + AgentSkill
- Tools re-export: src/core/tools/__init__.py

### Business Rules
- BR-RULE-054: list_accounts returns ONLY accounts accessible to authenticated agent (use list_for_agent)
- BR-RULE-055: list_accounts works without auth, returns empty array for unauthenticated

### Key Implementation Logic
- if not authenticated: return ListAccountsResponse(accounts=[])
- else: accounts = uow.accounts.list_for_agent(principal_id), convert ORM→schema

### ORM→Schema Conversion
- DB Account fields map directly to schema Account fields

title: Verify clean baseline
description:
Run quality gates BEFORE any code changes to confirm clean state.

## Instructions

1. **Run quality gate**:
   ```bash
   make quality 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```
   Record the exact pass count (e.g., "3481 passed").

2. **Start agent DB** (if integration tests are needed):
   ```bash
   eval $(.claude/skills/agent-db/agent-db.sh up)
   ```

## Gate — HARD STOP on any failure

If ANY test fails in the baseline, STOP. Do NOT proceed. Report the
exact failures and leave this atom OPEN.

## Record Baseline

```bash
bd update salesagent-i01 --append-notes "## Baseline
- make quality: [N] passed, 0 failed"
```
acceptance_criteria: make quality passes. Pass count recorded in epic notes.

title: Prep salesagent-b41
description:
Read the task and all affected code BEFORE making any changes.
This atom is READ-ONLY — do not edit any files.

## Instructions

1. **Read the task**:
   ```bash
   bd show salesagent-b41
   ```
   Read description, notes, design fields. Understand exactly what
   needs to change.

2. **Read every file** listed in the task description.
   For each test or source file, understand:
   - What does it currently do?
   - What should it do after the change?
   - What existing patterns does it follow?

3. **Identify dependencies**:
   - What _impl function(s) will be called?
   - What transport wrappers exist (MCP tool, A2A raw, REST route)?
   - What factories or fixtures are available?
   - What harness environments exist (ProductEnv, DeliveryPollEnv, etc.)?

4. **Write a brief plan** to the task notes:
   ```bash
   bd update salesagent-b41 --append-notes "## Prep
   - Files to modify: [list]
   - Production code to call: [_impl functions]
   - Transport routes: [MCP, A2A, REST identifiers]
   - Approach: [1-3 sentences]"
   ```

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT start editing files in this atom
- Do NOT skip reading affected files
- Do NOT assume you know the code without reading it
acceptance_criteria: Task read. All affected files read. Plan written to task notes.

title: Execute salesagent-b41
description:
Implement the changes described in the task and the prep plan.

## Instructions

1. **Re-read the plan** from `bd show salesagent-b41` (## Prep section).

2. **Make the changes** following the plan. For each change:
   - Follow CLAUDE.md critical patterns
   - Follow existing conventions in the file
   - Keep changes minimal — do only what the task asks

3. **Run quality gates**:
   ```bash
   make quality
   ```
   If it fails, fix the issue and re-run. Do NOT close this atom
   until make quality passes.

4. **Run targeted integration tests** (if the task involves DB):
   ```bash
   scripts/run-test.sh tests/integration/<relevant>.py -x -v 2>&1 | tail -10
   ```

5. **Record results**:
   ```bash
   bd update salesagent-b41 --append-notes "## Execution
   - Changes made: [summary]
   - make quality: PASS ([N] passed)"
   ```

## When EXISTING Tests Fail

If your changes break pre-existing tests:
1. STOP. Do NOT modify the failing tests to match your code.
2. Ask: is my change wrong, or is the test wrong?
3. If your change is wrong — revert and try a different approach.
4. If the test has a genuine bug — document WHY before modifying it.

Sacred rule: NEVER adjust tests to match code without justification.

## If Blocked

If you cannot complete the task:
```bash
bd update salesagent-b41 --append-notes "## BLOCKED
- [describe the blocker]
- [what was tried]"
```
Leave this atom OPEN for user direction.
acceptance_criteria: Changes implemented. make quality passes. Results recorded in task notes.

title: MCP auth chain drops testing_context (dry_run / test_session_id / mock_time)
description:
The FastMCP pipeline re-resolves identity from HTTP headers via the mcp_auth_middleware -> transport_helpers path. The headers carry x-adcp-auth (token) and x-adcp-tenant, but no mechanism exists to propagate AdCPTestContext fields (dry_run, test_session_id, mock_time, jump_to_event) from client to server.

Consequence: any test or client that relies on testing_context through MCP will silently skip it. The IMPL and A2A paths both preserve testing_context (because identity is passed in directly as a Python object). MCP does not.

## Discovered by

The cross-transport parity property test in
tests/integration/property/test_cross_transport_parity_property.py. When
run with dry_run=True and IMPL/A2A/MCP all dispatched through MediaBuyCreateEnv,
MCP raised 'Setup incomplete' validation errors at line 1316-1327 of
media_buy_create.py for every generated payload. IMPL and A2A (which see
testing_ctx.dry_run=True) correctly skipped the setup check. Hypothesis
output showed 0 passing / 8 failing examples.

## Expected

One of the following:

1. MCP protocol reserves headers for testing_context (e.g., x-adcp-dry-run,
   x-adcp-test-session-id) and the middleware reads them into the resolved
   identity.

2. testing_context travels as a top-level request field on the tool
   arguments and the wrapper layer moves it onto identity.

3. testing_context is server-side-only (env var / tenant flag) and the
   MCP client cannot request it -- doc this explicitly and remove the
   identity.testing_context propagation on the IMPL/A2A paths for
   consistency.

## Why this matters

Current test harness tests that dispatch through MCP with dry_run=True
cannot reach the business logic they intend to exercise -- they stop at
setup validation. Developer-facing: MCP clients cannot write idempotent
dry-run test suites against tenants that haven't completed setup.

## Acceptance criteria

- Cross-transport parity property passes with all three transports
- If protocol-level support: new integration test that sets a testing-context
  header through MCP and asserts the resolved identity.testing_context
  reflects it
- If architectural decision is 'not supported': documentation in
  docs/development/patterns-reference.md and code comment near
  mcp_auth_middleware.resolve_identity_from_context

title: Infra-5-fix: Revert production test backdoor, wire sidecar as real agent (18+6+2 tests)
description:
Infra-5 introduced a test-only code path in production. Revert and re-architect.

## What Happened
Commit 6e5bd621 added to src/core/creative_agent_registry.py:
    def _get_mock_formats():
        mock_url = os.environ.get('MOCK_CREATIVE_AGENT_URL')
        if mock_url:
            result = _fetch_from_mock_agent(mock_url)
            if result is not None:
                return result
        return [hardcoded 11 formats]

This is a test backdoor in production code. Problems:
1. MOCK_CREATIVE_AGENT_URL leaks to ALL test suites via test-stack.sh → tox pass_env
2. Integration tests that call _impl directly now get sidecar formats instead of hardcoded ones (6 regressions)
3. Sidecar state is shared across BDD scenarios (nondeterministic)
4. 2 e2e suite tests broke because format catalog shape changed

## The Fix — Three Parts

### Part 1: Revert production code change
src/core/creative_agent_registry.py — remove _fetch_from_mock_agent() and the MOCK_CREATIVE_AGENT_URL check in _get_mock_formats(). Production code must not have test-infrastructure conditionals.

### Part 2: Wire sidecar as a real creative agent
The Docker server should discover the sidecar through its normal agent registration path — the same way it discovers real creative agents. The sidecar IS a creative agent; Docker doesn't need to know it's controllable.

Investigation needed: how does the Docker server discover creative agents?
- If via env var (CREATIVE_AGENT_URLS or similar): add sidecar URL to the list
- If via DB (agent registry table): Given step creates the agent record via factory
- If via config file: mount a test config that includes the sidecar

The key: the server's code path for fetching formats from the sidecar must be IDENTICAL to its code path for fetching from a real agent. No special branches.

### Part 3: Given step is the single source of catalog state
Every BDD scenario that tests format filtering must:
1. Reset the sidecar catalog (POST /test/reset) in fixture setup
2. Seed specific formats (POST /test/set-formats) in the Given step
3. Assert against those specific formats in the Then step

The set_registry_formats(formats) method on CreativeFormatsEnv does the right thing per mode:
- In-process: patches mock registry to return these formats (already works)
- E2E: POSTs to sidecar (already works from Infra-5)

The missing piece: env._configure_mocks() must NOT seed default formats into the sidecar. Each scenario must seed its own. No defaults, no shared state.

### Part 4: Integration tests use harness, not _get_mock_formats()
tests/integration/test_get_products_filter_semantics.py calls _impl directly without a harness env. It relies on _get_mock_formats() returning hardcoded 11 formats. This is fragile — the test doesn't own its catalog state.

Fix: wrap these integration tests in CreativeFormatsEnv (which mocks the registry) and call env.set_registry_formats() with the specific formats each test needs. Remove dependency on _get_mock_formats() hardcoded values.

## Affected Tests
- 6 integration regressions in test_get_products_filter_semantics.py (Part 4)
- 7 BDD regressions in UC-005 [e2e_rest] (Part 3 — sidecar catalog mismatch)
- 2 e2e regressions in test_discovery_endpoints_e2e.py (Part 2 — format structure)
- 18 UC-005 BDD [e2e_rest] (Part 3 — some pass with wrong catalog, some fail)

## Reproduction
tox -e integration -- tests/integration/test_get_products_filter_semantics.py -x
tox -e bdd -- tests/bdd/test_uc005_discover_creative_formats.py -k e2e_rest -x

## Files to Modify
- src/core/creative_agent_registry.py (REVERT _fetch_from_mock_agent, remove MOCK_CREATIVE_AGENT_URL check)
- tests/integration/test_get_products_filter_semantics.py (wrap in CreativeFormatsEnv)
- tests/harness/creative_formats.py (_configure_mocks: no sidecar default seeding)
- docker-compose.e2e.yml (wire sidecar URL into agent discovery, not via special env var)
- scripts/test-stack.sh (keep MOCK_CREATIVE_AGENT_URL for sidecar HTTP admin, but NOT pass to production code)

## Core Invariant
Production code has zero test-only conditionals. Test state is owned by the scenario. Every format catalog assertion is deterministic because the Given step is the single source of truth about what formats exist.

## Principles Violated by Infra-5
1. No test-only code paths in production (MOCK_CREATIVE_AGENT_URL conditional)
2. No shared mutable state across scenarios (sidecar serves last test's leftovers)
3. No implicit environment coupling (env var leaks to integration suite)
4. Given step owns the state (integration tests rely on hardcoded _get_mock_formats)

title: E2E: DB UniqueViolation — tenant/data collision in Docker (10 scenarios)
description:
10 e2e_rest scenarios fail with UniqueViolation because the test creates data (tenant, product) that already exists in the Docker database from prior tests or Docker setup.

## Failing tests (all e2e_rest only)
- test_account_id_string_is_wrapped_in_account_object
- test_account_resolution_boundary__boundary_point
- test_campaign_ref_is_renamed_to_buyer_campaign_ref
- test_current_field_takes_precedence_over_deprecated
- test_profile_with_extra_metadata_fields_preserves_them
- test_profile_with_invalid_property_ids_falls_back_to_selection_type_all
- test_profile_with_only_publisher_domain_infers_selection_type_all
- test_profile_with_property_ids_infers_selection_type_by_id
- test_profile_with_property_tags_infers_selection_type_by_tag
- test_profile_with_selection_type_already_present_passes_through

## Root cause
E2E tests create tenant/product data via factories against Docker PostgreSQL. The Docker DB retains data across tests (no per-test isolation like integration_db). Multiple tests collide on the same tenant_id or product_id.

## Fix options
A. Use unique IDs per e2e test (uuid suffix)
B. xfail on e2e_rest with reason
C. Clean up Docker DB before each e2e test
notes:

---

# Context Bundle: test_account_id_string_is_wrapped_in_account_object

**Tags**: `T-COMPAT-001-account-id`
**Transports failing**: a2a, e2e_rest, impl, mcp, rest

---

## Error (VERBATIM)

sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError: (psycopg2.errors.UniqueViolation) duplicate key value violates unique constraint "tenants_pkey"
DETAIL:  Key (tenant_id)=(compat_tenant) already exists.

**Root cause**: The fixture creating `compat_tenant` runs more than once (likely per-transport or per-scenario) without a teardown or upsert guard. The step or fixture is calling `session.add()` on a tenant that already exists.

**Fix target**: Find the `Given`/background fixture that creates `compat_tenant` and make it idempotent — either use `get_or_create` semantics, scope the fixture to `session` instead of `function`, or use a factory with `create_sync` that handles conflicts.

---

## Gherkin Scenario (VERBATIM)

*File: `tests/bdd/features/BR-COMPAT-001-deprecated-field-normalization.feature`*

```gherkin
  @T-COMPAT-001-account-id
  Scenario: account_id string is wrapped in account object
    When the normalizer translates account_id "acc-456" for get_products
    Then the result contains account with account_id "acc-456"
    And the result does not contain account_id

---

## Orientation (VERBATIM — do this FIRST)

1. Read `.agent-index/INDEX.md` — this is the complete index of pre-computed type stubs.
   It tells you which file answers which question (harness envs, factories, schemas, errors).
2. Read the stub files listed in INDEX.md that are relevant to this UC.
3. Use `ast-grep --pattern '@then($_)' tests/bdd/steps/` to find existing step patterns.

DO NOT grep the codebase for harness methods. The `.agent-index` stubs are the complete API.
DO NOT invent mock hacks for methods not listed in the stubs.

---

## Conftest Harness Routing

The fixture creating tenant `compat_tenant` must be scoped so it runs **once per session** (or once per scenario with an idempotent guard), not once per transport. With 5 transports all executing the same scenario, the fixture fires 5 times against the same DB row.

Check `tests/bdd/conftest.py` (or the step file for this feature) for the fixture that inserts `compat_tenant`. Change its pytest scope to `session` or wrap the insert in a `SELECT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` / `get_or_create` pattern.

Full feature file: `tests/bdd/features/BR-COMPAT-001-deprecated-field-normalization.feature`

---

## Relevant Schema Fields

The `When` step exercises a **normalizer** (not a full DB-backed request). The normalizer receives a raw dict with `account_id: "acc-456"` and must return a dict where:
- `account.account_id == "acc-456"` is present
- top-level `account_id` key is absent

Full schema: `src/core/schemas.py` (look for `account` field and any `account_id` deprecation handling)

---

## Implementation Checklist

1. **Fix the fixture scope** — resolve the `UniqueViolation` before anything else; the assertion can't run while setup is crashing.
2. **Implement (or find) the `When` step**: `the normalizer translates account_id {string} for {word}` — this should call the normalizer/compatibility layer directly, no DB needed.
3. **Implement the `Then` steps**:
   - `the result contains account with account_id {string}` → assert `result["account"]["account_id"] == expected`
   - `the result does not contain account_id` → assert `"account_id" not in result`
4. Run the full scenario across all transports: `scripts/run-test.sh tests/bdd/ -k "account_id_string_is_wrapped_in_account_object" --no-header -q`
## Reproduction
- Local (4 transports impl/a2a/mcp/rest via integration_db): 60 passed, 0 failed for the 10 listed scenarios
- e2e_rest (requires Docker, not locally reproducible): UniqueViolation per bug report
- Prior fixes address 9/10:
  * 3 COMPAT tests → fixed by dqxo (compat_normalization step now uses env.setup_default_data + env.setup_product_chain)
  * 6 UC-GET-PRODUCTS profile tests: 0qx8 wired e2e_config BUT tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc_get_products_inventory.py:62-70 still has hardcoded TenantFactory(tenant_id='test_tenant'). Needs same fix as dqxo.
  * 1 UC-006 account_resolution_boundary: already uses ensure_tenant_principal → env.setup_default_data (tests/bdd/steps/generic/_account_resolution.py:16-21). Correct pattern.

## Remaining fix target
tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc_get_products_inventory.py:62-70 — given_tenant hardcodes tenant_id='test_tenant'. Replace with env.setup_default_data().

## Core Invariant
Same as dqxo: BDD Given steps must use env.setup_default_data() or env.setup_product_chain() for tenant/product creation; never TenantFactory(tenant_id='...') directly.
## Trace-similar
Only one remaining TenantFactory(...) call in tests/bdd/steps/ — uc_get_products_inventory.py:62-70 (given_tenant). All other BDD Given steps either use env.setup_default_data() or the shared ensure_tenant_principal helper. No additional systemic patterns.
## Architect Review
1. Root cause — LOW. Hardcoded TenantFactory(tenant_id='test_tenant') reused across transport parametrizations.
2. Fix minimality — LOW. 5-line step rewrite to delegate to env.setup_default_data(), matching dqxo.
3. Regression risk — LOW. In-process tests pass today; fix only scopes tenant_id to env uuid, downstream InventoryProfileFactory uses tenant object not id.
4. Architecture compliance — LOW. Matches tests/CLAUDE.md factory-via-harness rule.
5. Similar bug scalability — LOW. Last remaining hardcoded-tenant instance in tests/bdd/steps/.
6. Test coverage sufficiency — LOW. 24 in-process inventory_profile variants run per transport.

Verdict: ALL_LOW.
close_reason: All 10 scenarios addressed: 3 via dqxo, 6 via this fix + 0qx8, 1 already correct (UC-006)

title: Fix: UC-002 account resolution Pydantic validation errors (5 tests)
description:
5 UC-002 tests fail with CreateMediaBuyRequest Pydantic validation errors when account_not_found scenario runs.

## Failing tests
- test_account_not_found__explicit_account_id (5 transports)

## Error
pydantic_core.ValidationError: 5 validation errors for CreateMediaBuyRequest

## Root cause (suspected)
Main's #1175 (universal request normalization) changes how the account field is structured before Pydantic parses the request. The normalization may wrap account_id in an AccountReference dict in a way CreateMediaBuyRequest doesn't expect.

## Investigation
1. Check src/core/request_compat.py normalize functions
2. Check how account field flows: Given step → ctx → When step → request construction
3. Compare CreateMediaBuyRequest schema expectations vs what normalization produces
design:
## Root Cause
tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc002_create_media_buy.py:132-143 — given_account_id_not_found calls env.call_impl(account_ref=...). The harness passes kwargs to CreateMediaBuyRequest which requires buyer_ref/brand/start_time/end_time and rejects 'account_ref' (schema field is 'account').

Note: prior Given 'a valid create_media_buy request with account_id X' already populates ctx['request_kwargs']['account'] via _ensure_request_defaults — the full request is properly staged for the When step. The But step only needs to verify the DB precondition.

## Fix Approach
Replace given_account_id_not_found body with a DB-precondition check identical to given_account_not_exists (line 146 in same file): query AccountRepository.get_by_id and assert absence. Delete the call_impl anti-pattern.

Given both steps (line 132 and line 146) are preconditions and share logic, the minimal DRY fix is to have given_account_id_not_found delegate to given_account_not_exists, or share a helper.

## Core Invariant
Given steps must not dispatch requests (BDD no-request-in-Given). Preconditions verify state via direct repository/DB queries, not transport calls.
notes:
# Context Bundle: test_account_not_found__explicit_account_id

**Tags**: T-UC-002-ext-r
**Transports failing**: a2a, e2e_rest, impl, mcp, rest
**Agent index**: .agent-index/INDEX.md

---

## Agent Instructions (non-negotiable)
- Read `.agent-index/INDEX.md` FIRST — it tells you which stub files to read for this UC

- Use `ast-grep` to find existing step patterns: `ast-grep --pattern '@then($_)' tests/bdd/steps/`
- Run tests: `scripts/run-test.sh tests/bdd/ -k "test_account_not_found__explicit_account_id" --no-header -q`
- The assertion must match the Gherkin claim and the business rule invariant
- If production doesn't match the spec, add xfail with the reason — do NOT weaken the assertion

---

## Error (VERBATIM)
```
pydantic_core._pydantic_core.ValidationError: 5 validation errors for CreateMediaBuyRequest
buyer_ref
  Field required [type=missing, input_value={'account_ref': AccountRe..._id='acc-nonexistent'))}, input_type=dict]
    For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/v/missing
brand
  Field required [type=missing, input_value={'account_ref': AccountRe..._id='acc-nonexistent'))}, input_type=dict]
    For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/v/missing
start_ti
```

**Root cause**: The Given step constructs `CreateMediaBuyRequest` with only `account_ref`, omitting required fields (`buyer_ref`, `brand`, `start_time`, …). The request never reaches the account-lookup logic — it fails at Pydantic validation before the business rule can fire.

**Fix target**: The Given step `a valid create_media_buy request with account_id "acc-nonexistent"` must build a fully-valid request (all required fields populated) with `account.account_id = "acc-nonexistent"`. Use a factory or the harness helper that produces a valid baseline request, then override `account`.

---

## Gherkin Scenario (VERBATIM)
*File: tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-002-create-media-buy.feature*

```gherkin
  @T-UC-002-ext-r @extension @ext-r @error @post-f1 @post-f2 @post-f3
  Scenario: Account not found -- explicit account_id
    Given a valid create_media_buy request with account_id "acc-nonexistent"
    But the account_id does not exist in the seller's account store
    When the Buyer Agent sends the create_media_buy request
    Then the operation should fail
    And the error code should be "ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND"
    And the error recovery should be "terminal"
    And the error should include "suggestion" field
    And the suggestion should contain "list_accounts"
    # POST-F1: System state is unchanged on failure
    # POST-F2: Buyer knows what failed
    # POST-F3: Buyer knows to escalate (terminal)
```

---

## adcp-req Scenario (VERBATIM)
*Full feature file: tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-002-create-media-buy.feature*

```gherkin
  # @contextgit id=T-UC-002-ext-r-nk type=test upstream=[BR-UC-002-ext-r]
  @extension @ext-r @error
  Scenario: Account not found -- natural key
    Given a valid create_media_buy request with account natural key brand "unknown.com" operator "unknown.com"
    But no account matches the brand + operator combination
    When the Buyer Agent sends the create_media_buy request
    Then the operation should fail
    And the error code should be "ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND"
    And the error recovery should be "terminal"
    And the error should include "suggestion" field
```

---

## Relevant Schema Fields

**`CreateMediaBuyRequest`** — fields relevant to this scenario:
- `account` → `/schemas/core/account-ref.json` — carries `account_id` for explicit lookup
- `buyer_ref` (string, **required**)
- `brand` → `/schemas/core/brand-ref.json` (**required**)
- `start_time` → `/schemas/core/start-timing.json` (**required**)

Full schema: `tests/bdd/features/schemas/create-media-buy-request.json`

**Expected error shape** (from Gherkin assertions):
- `error_code` == `"ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND"`
- `recovery` == `"terminal"`
- response includes `"suggestion"` field
- `suggestion` contains `"list_accounts"`

---

## Orientation (VERBATIM — do this FIRST)

1. Read `.agent-index/INDEX.md` — this is the complete index of pre-computed type stubs.
   It tells you which file answers which question (harness envs, factories, schemas, errors).
2. Read the stub files listed in INDEX.md that are relevant to this UC.
3. Use `ast-grep --pattern '@then($_)' tests/bdd/steps/` to find existing step patterns.

DO NOT grep the codebase for harness methods. The .agent-index stubs are the complete API.
DO NOT invent mock hacks for methods not listed in the stubs.

---

## Conftest Harness Routing (VERBATIM — which env class is used for this UC)

```python
uc == "UC-002":
        marker_names = {m.name for m in request.node.iter_markers()}
        if "account" in marker_names:
            extra = _setup_db(request, ctx)

---

## Step File to Modify
`tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc002_create_media_buy.py`

Steps to implement / fix:
1. **Given** `a valid create_media_buy request with account_id "acc-nonexistent"` — must build a fully-valid request with all required fields, overriding `account.account_id` to the given value. Do not construct `CreateMediaBuyRequest` with only the account field.
2. **But** `the account_id does not exist in the seller's account store` — likely a no-op (the account truly doesn't exist; no DB setup needed) or may need harness support to ensure no matching account is seeded.
3. **Then** `the error code should be "ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND"` — assert `response.error_code == "ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND"` (exact string).
4. **And** `the error recovery should be "terminal"` — assert `response.recovery == "terminal"`.
5. **And** `the error should include "suggestion" field` — assert `"suggestion"` key is present in the error.
6. **And** `the suggestion should contain "list_accounts"` — assert `"list_accounts"` appears in the suggestion value.
## Reproduction (confirmed)
- Failing tests: tests/bdd/test_uc002_create_media_buy.py::test_account_not_found__explicit_account_id × 4 non-e2e transports (e2e = 5th)
- Error site: tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc002_create_media_buy.py:132-143 (given_account_id_not_found)
- The step calls env.call_impl(account_ref=ctx["account_ref"]). MediaBuyCreateEnv.call_impl builds CreateMediaBuyRequest(**kwargs) (harness/media_buy_create.py:164), which fails with 5 missing-field errors (buyer_ref, brand, start_time, end_time) + 'account_ref' being 'extra_forbidden' (schema uses 'account').

## Root cause
given_account_id_not_found is a PRECONDITION step but incorrectly dispatches through env.call_impl. It also anti-patterns — Given should not send requests (BDD invariant). The companion step given_account_not_exists (same file, line 146) does the correct thing: uses AccountRepository to verify no matching account exists. The broken step should mirror that pattern.
## Trace-similar
AST scan of tests/bdd/steps/ for Given/Then steps calling call_impl/call_mcp/call_a2a/_dispatch:
- Only one Given violator: given_account_id_not_found (the bug itself)
- Then step dispatches in uc002_nfr.py (5 steps) are intentional NFR measurement dispatches (auth-before-business-logic, rate_limiting, payload_size, sla, budget_validated) — different domain, separate concern.
No systemic pattern to file.
## Architect Review

1. Root cause correctness — LOW. Given step dispatches through call_impl with only account_ref; harness constructs full CreateMediaBuyRequest and fails on missing required fields. Reproduction is deterministic.
2. Fix minimality — LOW. Replace given_account_id_not_found body to mirror given_account_not_exists (same file, DB-only precondition check). No harness, factory, or production changes.
3. Regression risk — LOW. The step currently fails hard on all transports. Any alternate invocation (no 'But' step → no call) is unaffected. DB-check path matches the sibling natural-key step which currently passes.
4. Architecture compliance — LOW. Aligns with BDD invariant 'Given steps must not dispatch requests' (test_architecture_bdd_no_request_in_then also bans this in Then; Given-dispatch is analogous anti-pattern). The anti-pattern comment 'TRANSPORT-BYPASS' in the current code is misleading: it's still dispatching _impl, just skipping transport wrappers.
5. Similar bug scalability — LOW. AST trace found no other Given-step dispatch. NFR Then steps are measurement-specific and out of scope.
6. Test coverage sufficiency — LOW. The existing scenario (test_account_not_found__explicit_account_id × all transports) already covers the precondition + failure path. When step handles dispatch; Then steps assert error code + recovery + suggestion. No new tests needed — fix-in-place restores the scenario.

Verdict: ALL_LOW. Proceed to fix.
## E2E Verification
Fix is in a Given (precondition) step that runs before any transport dispatch. The step is transport-agnostic — it checks AccountRepository directly via the DB session. 4/5 transports verified locally (impl/a2a/mcp/rest all pass). e2e_rest also uses the same Given step machinery; no transport-specific code path differs. Full Docker verification not required for this class of fix.
close_reason: Bug fixed: Given step no longer dispatches; 4/4 non-e2e transports pass; zero regressions

title: Fix: UC-019 REST get_media_buys returns empty/HTML instead of JSON (6 tests)
description:
6 UC-019 tests fail on REST transport with 'Expecting value: line 1 column 1' — the REST endpoint returns empty body or HTML instead of JSON.

## Failing tests (all [rest] transport)
- test_query_media_buys_by_buyer_refs
- test_query_media_buys_by_specific_media_buy_ids
- test_query_returns_empty_results_when_principal_has_no_matching_media_buys
- test_principal_scoping__partition (2 variants)
- test_inv1_holds__no_snapshot_fields_when_not_requested

## Root cause (suspected)
Main's #1175 (universal request normalization) may have changed how the REST route handles the get_media_buys request body. Or the REST endpoint itself is returning wrong content-type.

## Investigation
1. Check src/routes/api_v1.py get_media_buys route
2. Check if request normalization middleware intercepts and breaks the body
3. Run manually: curl the REST endpoint with a valid body

## Error (VERBATIM)
AssertionError: Expected a response but got error: 4 validation errors for GetMediaBuysRequest
account.AccountReference1.account_id
  Input should be a valid string [type=string_type, input_value=None, input_type=NoneType]
    For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/v/string_type
account.AccountReference2.brand
  Field required [type=missing, input_value={'account_id': None}, input_type=dict]
    For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/v/missing

**Root cause**: The When step is passing `account=None` (or `account={"account_id": None}`) to `GetMediaBuysRequest`. The `account` field must either be omitted entirely or be a valid `AccountReference` union member. For this test — which only filters by `buyer_refs` — `account` should not be set at all.

---

## Gherkin Scenario (VERBATIM)
*File: `tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-019-query-media-buys.feature`*

```gherkin
@T-UC-019-main-filter-refs @main-flow @filtering
Scenario: Query media buys by buyer_refs
  Given the principal "buyer-001" owns media buy "mb-001" with buyer_ref "campaign-alpha"
  And the principal "buyer-001" owns media buy "mb-002" with buyer_ref "campaign-beta"
  When the Buyer Agent sends a get_media_buys request with buyer_refs ["campaign-alpha"]
  Then the response should include media buy "mb-001"
  And the response should not include media buy "mb-002"
  # POST-S6: buyer_ref used for filtering and correlation

Full feature file: `tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-019-query-media-buys.feature`

---

## Relevant Schema Fields

**`GetMediaBuysRequest`** — only fields relevant to this scenario:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `account` | `AccountReference \| None` | **MUST be omitted or a valid union member** — never `None` or `{"account_id": None}` |
| `buyer_refs` | `list[str] \| None` | Pass `["campaign-alpha"]` for this test |

`AccountReference` is a discriminated union with two variants:
- `AccountReference1` requires `account_id: str` (non-null)
- `AccountReference2` requires `brand: str`

**Fix**: Do not pass `account` at all when the step does not mention it. Use `buyer_refs=["campaign-alpha"]` only.

Full schema: `src/core/schemas.py` (search `GetMediaBuysRequest`) and `get-media-buys-request.json`

---

## What to Fix

In `tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc019_query_media_buys.py`, find the When step:

When the Buyer Agent sends a get_media_buys request with buyer_refs ["campaign-alpha"]

The step implementation is constructing the request with `account=None` (or an empty account dict). The fix is to build `GetMediaBuysRequest` with **only** `buyer_refs` populated and `account` omitted entirely:

```python

# CORRECT — account is not part of this step's contract

- `the response should not include media buy "mb-002"` → assert `"mb-002"` does NOT appear

      | both account_id and brand/operator present      | {"account_id": "acc_001", "brand": {"domain": "x.com"}, "operator": "x"}  | the error should be INVALID_REQUEST with suggestion           |

---

## Business Rule Invariants

The two failing rows represent **input validation** errors — they must be rejected before any account lookup:

1. **Account field absent**: If the `account` field is entirely omitted from the sync request, the request is structurally invalid → `INVALID_REQUEST`.
2. **Both account_id and brand/operator present**: Providing `account_id` together with `brand`/`operator` is mutually exclusive — specifying both is ambiguous → `INVALID_REQUEST`.

These are pre-resolution checks (schema/structural validation), not account-lookup errors. The production code must raise `INVALID_REQUEST` before attempting any DB lookup.

*Find full business rules in the upstream spec: search for `BR-UC-006` in `tests/bdd/features/`.*

---

## Relevant Schema Fields

Fields on the sync-creative request relevant to these two rows:

account:
  account_id: str | None        # mutually exclusive with brand+operator
  brand:
    domain: str | None
  operator: str | None          # mutually exclusive with account_id

**Invariant**: `account_id` and `brand`/`operator` are mutually exclusive. Absent `account` field → `INVALID_REQUEST`. Both paths present → `INVALID_REQUEST`.

Full schema: read the relevant `.agent-index/` stub (see `INDEX.md` for the UC-006 entry).

---

## Orientation (MANDATORY — do this FIRST)

1. Read `.agent-index/INDEX.md` — this is the complete index of pre-computed type stubs.
   It tells you which file answers which question (harness envs, factories, schemas, errors).
2. Read the stub files listed in INDEX.md that are relevant to this UC.
3. Use `ast-grep --pattern '@then($_)' tests/bdd/steps/` to find existing step patterns.

DO NOT grep the codebase for harness methods. The .agent-index stubs are the complete API.
DO NOT invent mock hacks for methods not listed in the stubs.

---

## Conftest Harness Routing (VERBATIM)

```python
uc == "UC-006":
        marker_names = {m.name for m in request.node.iter_markers()}
        if "account" in marker_names:
            extra = _setup_db(request, ctx)
            from tests.harness.creative_sync import CreativeSyncEnv

            with CreativeSyncEnv(**extra) as env:
                ctx["env"] = env
                yield
        else:
            yield

These failing tests carry `@account` → they use `CreativeSyncEnv`. Read the stub for `CreativeSyncEnv` in `.agent-index/` to see what methods it exposes for asserting error codes.

---

## What to Fix

The `Then` step for `the error should be INVALID_REQUEST with suggestion` either:
- Does not exist yet in `tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc006_sync_creatives.py`, or
- Exists but asserts on the wrong attribute / wrong error code spelling.

The assertion must:
1. Read the recorded error from `ctx["env"]` (via the harness API — check the stub)
2. Assert `error_code == "INVALID_REQUEST"` (exact string — do not weaken to truthiness)
3. Assert a suggestion is present

Do NOT assert on request data inside a `Then` step (architecture guard: `test_architecture_bdd_no_request_in_then.py`).
## Findings
- Scenario: @T-UC-006-boundary-account outline; e2e_rest fails on two success-path partitions (account_id present + active; brand+operator+single match). Local 4 transports pass.
- Root cause (per bug description): account data collision or Docker-side account resolution mismatch in the 2 success-path partitions. Failure surface not reproducible locally.

## Core Invariant
Success-path partitions depend on specific account records existing in the Docker DB. Without E2E-side account fixture injection, these partitions can't run.

## Fix
Selective xfail(strict=False) on e2e_rest for T-UC-006-boundary-account gated to the two failing boundary_point substrings.

title: UC-005-real-catalog: Feed real creative agent formats into registry mock (18 BDD E2E + stronger in-process tests)
description:
Replace fake 1-2 format mock data with the real 49-format catalog from the creative agent container. The mock mechanism stays (it correctly isolates the seller's filter logic from the external HTTP call). The mock DATA changes from fantasy to reality.

## Problem
UC-005 BDD tests mock the creative agent registry and feed it 1-3 fake formats built via FormatFactory. The seller's filter logic runs against this trivial input. Any filter 'works' against 1 format. The tests verify nothing meaningful about filtering.

In E2E mode, Docker fetches 49 real formats from the creative agent container. The filter runs against real data. But the BDD Then assertions expect the mock-controlled counts ('expected 1 format') and fail with 'got 49'.

## Root Cause
The mock DATA doesn't match reality. The mock MECHANISM is correct — it replaces the external HTTP call (system boundary) while the seller's filter code runs for real.

## Architecture Decision
The creative agent is an external system. The seller's list_creative_formats_impl does:
1. Fetch formats from creative agents via MCP (system boundary — external HTTP)
2. Filter in memory (seller's own logic — lines 231-325 of creative_formats.py)
3. Return filtered list

Step 1 is the mock target. Steps 2-3 are the code under test. This is correct. The mock replaces the boundary, not the logic.

What's wrong: the mock returns 1-2 fake Format objects. The real agent returns 49 with real types, dimensions, assets, renders. The filter logic is never exercised against realistic data.

## Fix

### 1. Fetch real catalog once
In the Background step 'at least one creative agent is registered with format definitions' (tests/bdd/steps/generic/given_entities.py:74):

    @given('at least one creative agent is registered with format definitions')
    def given_creative_agent_registered(ctx):
        import json
        from pathlib import Path
        
        catalog_path = Path(__file__).parents[4] / '.creative-agent-catalog.json'
        if catalog_path.exists():
            with open(catalog_path) as f:
                raw = json.load(f)
            # Convert JSON dicts to real Format objects
            real_formats = [_build_format_from_catalog_entry(entry) for entry in raw]
            ctx['env'].set_registry_formats(real_formats)
            ctx['real_catalog'] = real_formats
        
        ctx['creative_agents_registered'] = True

The .creative-agent-catalog.json file is already committed (49 formats fetched from the Docker creative agent container). It has format_id, name, type, is_responsive, renders, asset_keys for each format.

A helper _build_format_from_catalog_entry converts each JSON dict into a real Format object (using FormatFactory or direct construction).

### 2. Update Given steps that seed specific formats
Given steps like 'the creative agent registry has formats across multiple categories' currently build 3 fake formats. They should become ASSERTIONS on the real catalog:

    @given('the creative agent registry has formats across multiple categories')
    def given_registry_multi_categories(ctx):
        types = {str(f.type).split('.')[-1] for f in ctx['real_catalog']}
        assert len(types) >= 3, f'Real catalog has {types} — expected multiple categories'

The real catalog has display (28), video (12), dooh (4), audio (3), native (2) — 5 categories. The assertion passes. No fake data.

### 3. Update Then assertion counts
Then steps that assert exact counts need updating. For example:

    # OLD: mock had exactly 1 display format
    assert len(response.formats) == 1
    
    # NEW: real catalog has 28 display formats  
    expected_display = [f for f in ctx['real_catalog'] if str(f.type).split('.')[-1] == 'display']
    assert len(response.formats) == len(expected_display)

The count is DERIVED from the real catalog, not hardcoded. If the creative agent adds a format, .creative-agent-catalog.json is regenerated and the count updates automatically.

### 4. Handle 'empty catalog' scenarios
Gherkin scenario 'no creative agents have any registered formats' — the real agent always has 49 formats. This scenario tests 'what if there are NO formats at all.'

Fix: set_registry_formats([]) — an empty list. This is a valid test case: 'the seller has no creative agents, so no formats.' The mock returns empty. The filter returns empty. The assertion 'expected 0' passes.

This is NOT fake data — it's a real scenario where a seller hasn't registered any creative agents.

### 5. Handle format-specific Given steps
Given steps like 'a seller with known format IDs in the catalog' need to reference real format IDs from the catalog, not fake ones:

    @given('a seller with known format IDs in the catalog')
    def given_seller_known_ids(ctx):
        # Pick 2 real format IDs from the catalog
        catalog = ctx['real_catalog']
        ctx['known_format_ids'] = [f.format_id for f in catalog[:2]]

### 6. Refresh .creative-agent-catalog.json
Add a script or make target to regenerate the catalog from the running container:

    make refresh-creative-catalog
    # Starts Docker if needed, fetches formats, saves to .creative-agent-catalog.json

Run this when the creative agent version changes. Commit the updated file.

## What Stays Unchanged
- EXTERNAL_PATCHES['registry'] — the mock mechanism stays
- set_registry_formats() — the method stays (feeds Format objects into mock)
- When steps — unchanged (apply filters, dispatch)
- The filter code in _list_creative_formats_impl (lines 231-325) — unchanged, runs for real
- Integration tests that use set_registry_formats with FormatFactory — update to use real catalog

## Files to Modify
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/given_entities.py (Background step feeds real catalog, Given steps become assertions)
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/then_payload.py (Then counts derived from ctx['real_catalog'])
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/_registry.py (helper to load and convert catalog JSON)
- .creative-agent-catalog.json (already committed — may need richer fields for Format construction)

## Files NOT Modified
- tests/harness/creative_formats.py (mock stays, set_registry_formats stays)
- src/core/tools/creative_formats.py (production code untouched)
- src/core/creative_agent_registry.py (production code untouched)
- docker-compose.e2e.yml (infrastructure stays)

## Integration Tests (13 files)
Same approach: replace FormatFactory.build() data with real catalog entries. Each test file fetches the catalog once at module scope and uses real Format objects.

This can be a SEPARATE follow-up task — the BDD fix is higher priority.

## Verification
source .test-stack.env && tox -e bdd -- tests/bdd/test_uc005_discover_creative_formats.py -x

Expected: all UC-005 scenarios pass across all 5 transports with strong assertions against real 49-format catalog.

## Real Catalog Summary (from .creative-agent-catalog.json)
49 formats: 28 display, 12 video, 4 dooh, 3 audio, 2 native
Format IDs include: display_generative, display_300x250_image, video_standard, native_standard, audio_standard_15s, dooh_billboard_1920x1080, etc.

## Core Invariant
Mock replaces the system boundary (HTTP to creative agent). Mock DATA matches production reality (49 real formats). The seller's filter logic is tested against the same data it processes in production.
close_reason: Real 49-format catalog in BDD steps, zero regressions

title: AccountRepository + AccountUoW
description:
Implement AccountRepository following MediaBuyRepository pattern. Add AccountUoW to uow.py. Methods: get_by_id, get_by_natural_key, list_for_agent, upsert_from_sync, update_status, deactivate_missing, grant_access, has_access, resolve_reference.

Files: src/core/database/repositories/account.py, src/core/database/repositories/uow.py
design:
## Core Invariant
All account data access goes through AccountRepository with mandatory tenant_id scoping — no raw select(Account) or session.add(Account) outside the repository.

## Architecture Decisions
1. Single repository file with AccountRepository handling both Account and AgentAccountAccess (following CreativeRepository pattern)
2. AccountUoW with single accounts attribute (AccountRepository handles access table internally)
3. get_by_natural_key queries operator + brand JSON column — uses PostgreSQL JSONB operators

## Implementation Plan
1. Create src/core/database/repositories/account.py with AccountRepository:
   - __init__(session, tenant_id)
   - get_by_id(account_id) -> Account | None
   - get_by_natural_key(operator, brand_domain, brand_id=None) -> Account | None
   - list_for_agent(principal_id) -> list[Account] (joins AgentAccountAccess)
   - list_all(*, status=None) -> list[Account]
   - create(account: Account) -> Account (flush, not commit)
   - update_status(account_id, status) -> Account | None
   - update_fields(account_id, **kwargs) -> Account | None
   - grant_access(principal_id, account_id) -> AgentAccountAccess
   - revoke_access(principal_id, account_id) -> bool
   - has_access(principal_id, account_id) -> bool
   - list_accessible_account_ids(principal_id) -> list[str]
   - resolve_reference(ref: AccountReference) -> Account | None
2. Add AccountUoW to uow.py following MediaBuyUoW pattern
3. Register in repositories/__init__.py
4. Integration tests in tests/integration/test_account_repository.py
notes:
## Findings

### Repository Pattern (from MediaBuyRepository)
- Constructor: (session: Session, tenant_id: str), stored as _session/_tenant_id
- All queries include tenant_id scoping
- Writes: session.add() + flush(), never commit (UoW's job)
- _IMMUTABLE_FIELDS frozenset guards PKs/audit columns
- Methods return ORM models or None

### UoW Pattern (from BaseUoW in uow.py)
- __init__(tenant_id), __enter__ calls get_db_session(), __exit__ commits
- Abstract _init_repos() and _clear_repos()
- CreativeUoW has 2 repos (CreativeRepository + CreativeAssignmentRepository) — analogous to Account + AgentAccountAccess

### Junction Table Pattern (CreativeAssignmentRepository)
- get_by_creative, get_by_media_buy, get_existing (composite key)
- create(**kwargs), delete(id) — for AgentAccountAccess: grant/revoke/has_access

### Relevant Code
- src/core/database/repositories/media_buy.py — reference implementation
- src/core/database/repositories/uow.py — BaseUoW + all concrete UoWs
- src/core/database/repositories/creative.py:322-477 — junction table pattern
- src/core/database/repositories/__init__.py — exports
- tests/integration/test_tenant_config_repository.py — test pattern (factories + IntegrationEnv)

### Test Pattern
- _RepoEnv(IntegrationEnv) with EXTERNAL_PATCHES = {}
- Create data with factories, get session, construct repo, assert
- Test categories: happy path, tenant isolation, empty results, write roundtrip
## Architect Review
- [LOW] get_by_natural_key JSONB query: brand is JSONType. Use PostgreSQL JSON operators (brand->>'domain') for the query. This is well-supported by SQLAlchemy.
- [LOW] resolve_reference needs to handle RootModel AccountReference. Extract account_id from variant 1, or (brand, operator) from variant 2. Use existing adcp accessor pattern.
- [LOW] _IMMUTABLE_FIELDS should include tenant_id, account_id, created_at.

Overall: ALL_LOW
## Triage
Clean review (ALL_LOW) — proceeding to implement.
## Regression Test
- Test: tests/integration/test_account_repository.py (5 test classes, 6 tests)
- Status: FAILING (ModuleNotFoundError — repository not yet created)
- Tests: get_by_id, tenant isolation, list_for_agent, grant/has_access, UoW lifecycle
close_reason: AccountRepository + AccountUoW implemented with 6 integration tests

title: Rewrite-formats: test_creative_formats_mcp_filters — remove set_registry_formats (13 calls)
description:
Rewrite tests/integration/test_creative_formats_mcp_filters.py to work against the real creative agent catalog (49 formats from Docker container). Remove all set_registry_formats() calls.

## Task
Remove every call to env.set_registry_formats(). The creative agent container serves a real 49-format catalog. Tests must assert on filtering BEHAVIOR against real data, not against injected fake data.

## Real Catalog Reference
Read .creative-agent-catalog.json for the full catalog (49 formats: 28 display, 12 video, 4 dooh, 3 audio, 2 native). Each entry has format_id, name, type, is_responsive, renders, asset_keys.

## Pattern for Rewriting

OLD (fake data):
    formats = [FormatFactory.build(name='test-display', type=display)]
    env.set_registry_formats(formats)
    response = env.call_impl(req=ListCreativeFormatsRequest(type='display'))
    assert len(response.formats) == 1

NEW (real data):
    response = env.call_impl(req=ListCreativeFormatsRequest(type='display'))
    assert len(response.formats) > 0
    assert all(f.type.value == 'display' for f in response.formats)

For tests that verify specific counts, use the real catalog counts:
    # Real catalog: 28 display, 12 video, 4 dooh, 3 audio, 2 native
    response = env.call_impl(req=ListCreativeFormatsRequest(type='display'))
    assert len(response.formats) == 28  # or >= 1 for stability

For 'empty result' tests (no matching formats):
    # Use a filter that genuinely returns 0 from the real catalog
